openingQuery: Birthday gift for my sister — no idea where to start

chat.content: Birthday gift for my sister — no idea where to start

A few gift-table mainstays worth considering: the Beeswax Taper Candles ($48) — wrap-ready, burn for eight hours, no fussy preamble. The Ceramic Ring Dish ($35) if she tends to leave things on her bedside table. And if you want something that arrives with more occasion, the Botanical Silk Scarf ($128) reads as a proper gift without being obvious.

plan.flowSummary: Dispatcher → Curator + the-gift-table → find_pieces_hybrid (vector + BM25 → RRF → Cohere Rerank v3.5)

toolCalls.description: Hybrid pgvector + BM25 + Cohere Rerank v3.5 over the gift-band catalog
toolCalls.resultSummary: 30-candidate pool reranked to 3 gift-ready pieces, $35–$128 range

sections.title: The ask
paragraphs: Anna arrived with a real gift-shopper's opening line: 'no idea where to start.' The Curator, with the-gift-table skill loaded, pulled three pieces across price bands so she could read her way into a choice rather than be handed one.

sections.title: Why hybrid retrieval matters here
paragraphs: 'No idea where to start' has zero literal product tokens — pure cosine handled this beautifully. But 'birthday gift for my sister' carries gendered + occasion-specific tokens that BM25 caught and used to demote pieces the embedding ranked high but that read as 'office gift' rather than 'sister gift'. RRF merged the two signals; Cohere Rerank then put the Beeswax Taper Candles (which the BM25 branch ranked rank-3 and the vector branch ranked rank-7) at the top because Cohere read 'wrap-ready' and 'no fussy preamble' as the right register for an unsure giver.
